questions.question: They are cast or forged to form:

explanations.a: Connecting rods are generally forged to form an H-section near the small end for strength.
explanations.b: I-section is not formed near the small end.
explanations.c: H-section is not specifically formed near the big end.
explanations.d: A correct structural form is mentioned.

questions.question: Which shape provides greater strength to resist stresses than a solid rod of the same mass?

answers.a: H near the small end and an I near the big end
answers.b: I near the small end and an H near the big end

explanations.a: This combination provides high strength and stiffness with reduced weight.
explanations.b: This arrangement is not commonly used.
explanations.c: Only one option correctly describes the design.

questions.question: Failure of a connecting rod is commonly called:

explanations.a: Failure of a connecting rod is commonly referred to as throwing a rod.
explanations.b: Flowing of rod is not a recognized term.

questions.question: The grain pattern of the body or neck of the connecting rod is in:

explanations.a: The body or neck of the connecting rod is manufactured with vertical grain flow for strength.
explanations.b: Horizontal grain flow is used for the cap.
explanations.c: Inclined grain flow is not used.

questions.question: The grain pattern of the connecting rod cap is made in:

explanations.a: The cap is manufactured with horizontal grain flow to maintain roundness under high stress.
explanations.b: Vertical grain flow is used in the rod body, not the cap.
explanations.c: Inclined grain flow is not used.
